text:
Market today: Tech is bouncing after the AI/chip selloff, but the recovery is fragile. The real driver is sentiment around AI spending and energy costs — both directly hit your book. Chip names are trying to stabilize but haven't proven the dip is over.

Your holdings: AVGO (your largest, -12.89%) is flagged TRIM — it's underwater and a chip selloff hits it hardest, especially on margin. PLTR (-10.31%) is flagged SELL — double-digit loss with no clear floor yet, margin amplifies this pain fast. SPCX is brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no price history — manage it manually and keep size small; margin and new IPOs are a dangerous combo.

Watch: (1) Whether today's tech rebound holds into the close or fades — a failed bounce would hurt AVGO, PLTR, and CRWD further. (2) The congressional energy-cost bill for AI data centers — if it gains traction, it pressures the whole AI infrastructure trade.

Bottom line: Your two biggest fires are AVGO and PLTR — decide today whether you trim/exit before margin turns a bad position into a forced one.
